name: hermit-cell
summary: The cell of a hermit — a single devotional recluse's dwelling. Not a monastery cell but a standalone hut or cave-chamber. A little worship, a little sleeping, a little eating, all in one cramped sacred space.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: small, near-square — a hut's worth of room

primary_palette.note: a personal idol in the corner, the rest of the space for living
a weathered shrine-stone brought inside, worked into the dwelling
a small altar and a bedroll on the floor — no other concessions
a bone-shrine of personal ancestors or saints — private veneration

story_prompts: the hermit at home — stirring a small pot over a hearth, the shrine lit at the back
the hermit gone — a long absence; the candle a stub, the bowl empty, the bedroll neat
the hermit dead — a body at the kneeler, the idol watching; days old
a visitor welcomed — two bowls, two cups, evidence of an unexpected guest
the hermit slipping — notes pinned everywhere, the shrine overgrown with private sigils

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: very low and warm (0.15-0.25) — a hearth or one or two candles carrying the whole room

anti_patterns: the shrine and the living space must share the room — don't partition them
always lived-in — crumbs, ashes, a cloak hung; never museum-clean
small furniture only — no pews, no thrones, no wide pillars
